narrative_summary: The BRCA2 c.8067T>A (p.Cys2689*) variant, also reported as C2689X or Cys2689Ter, creates a premature stop codon in the BRCA2 protein and has been observed in individuals with hereditary breast and ovarian cancer as well as other malignancies such as prostate cancer and melanoma [PMC9024354]. Clinical evidence includes a co‑segregation analysis that yielded a likelihood ratio of 12.24 in favor of pathogenicity, indicating strong familial segregation with disease [PMC2714556]. The same variant was classified as pathogenic in a Brazilian breast‑cancer cohort and was absent from both the Brazilian non‑cancer control set and the gnomAD population databases, underscoring its rarity and disease association [PMC8907244]. Additional reports have identified the truncating change in diverse cancer screening programs, including a South African cohort of nearly 3,000 patients and a diagnostic workflow for Fanconi anemia, further supporting its relevance to cancer predisposition [PMC9024354, PMC3374947]. No experimental functional assays were presented in the extracted literature, and computational predictive scores were not reported. Population data consistently show the variant to be extremely rare or absent in large reference datasets, reinforcing its likely deleterious nature [PMC8907244]. Taken together, the convergent clinical classifications, segregation data, and absence from population controls provide robust evidence that the BRCA2 p.Cys2689* truncating variant is pathogenic and contributes to hereditary cancer risk.

article_mentions.overall_article_summary: The study screened nearly 3,000 South African breast and ovarian cancer patients, identified 481 likely‑pathogenic BRCA1/2 variants, and catalogued recurrent and novel mutations—including the BRCA2 p.Cys2689Ter truncating variant—across diverse ethnic groups.
article_mentions.title: Prevalence of Clinically Relevant Germline BRCA Variants in a Large Unselected South African Breast and Ovarian Cancer Cohort: A Public Sector Experience

article_mentions.overall_article_summary: The article describes a simple co‑segregation analysis method and reports a likelihood ratio of 12.24 for BRCA2 p.Cys2689X, indicating evidence in favor of pathogenicity for this variant.
article_mentions.title: A simple method for co-segregation analysis to evaluate the pathogenicity of unclassified variants; BRCA1 and BRCA2 as an example

article_mentions.overall_article_summary: The study identifies the BRCA2 p.Cys2689* (c.8067T>A) stop‑gain variant as pathogenic in the Brazilian breast cancer cohort.
article_mentions.title: Detection of germline variants in Brazilian breast cancer patients using multigene panel testing

article_mentions.overall_article_summary: The study presents an NGS‑based diagnostic workflow for Fanconi anemia and reports the detection of a BRCA2 nonsense variant (c.T8067A, p.C2689X) in a patient cohort.
article_mentions.title: Diagnosis of Fanconi Anemia: Mutation Analysis by Next-Generation Sequencing

article_mentions.overall_article_summary: Women with DCIS who have a family history of ovarian cancer or a BRCAPRO probability ≥10% show a high rate of BRCA1/2 mutations, indicating they are appropriate candidates for genetic testing despite the absence of invasive disease.
article_mentions.title: Predictive Factors for BRCA1/BRCA2 Mutations in Women With Ductal Carcinoma In Situ

article_mentions.overall_article_summary: The paper presents an embryo‑tracking system that enhances the safety, efficiency, and scalability of sequencing‑based preimplantation genetic testing without affecting the genomic profile of embryos.
article_mentions.title: Embryo tracking system for high-throughput sequencing-based preimplantation genetic testing

article_mentions.overall_article_summary: The article characterizes the somatic mutation landscape of breast cancers from CHEK2 germline mutation carriers, noting hormone‑receptor positivity and lack of HRD signatures, and includes a table listing various somatic alterations such as a BRCA2 nonsense variant (p.C2689*).',
article_mentions.title: The Landscape of Somatic Genetic Alterations in Breast Cancers from CHEK2 Germline Mutation Carriers

article_mentions.overall_article_summary: The study presents a large pan‑cancer whole‑genome analysis of 2,520 metastatic tumours, revealing driver mutations—including a nonsense BRCA2 p.Cys2689* variant—in core cancer predisposition genes and highlighting the potential for genomic profiling to guide therapy.
article_mentions.title: Pan-cancer whole-genome analyses of metastatic solid tumours
